QF.id: VI Functions of DP Account Opening_51

question.text: An Accredited Investor has annual income of Rs. 1.2 crore and net worth of Rs. 6 crore, of which Rs. 3 crore is in financial assets. Does this person qualify as an Accredited Investor?

answer.text: Yes, under the combined criteria: income ≥ Rs. 1 crore AND net worth ≥ Rs. 5 crore with at least Rs. 2.5 crore in financial assets
No, because income is below Rs. 2 crore
No, because net worth is below Rs. 7.5 crore
No, because financial assets are below Rs. 3.75 crore

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The third criterion: income ≥ Rs. 1 Cr (met: 1.2 Cr) AND net worth ≥ Rs. 5 Cr (met: 6 Cr) with financial assets ≥ Rs. 2.5 Cr (met: 3 Cr). All conditions satisfied. **The Trap:** 'Income below Rs. 2 crore' only disqualifies under the first criterion, not the third. The three criteria are alternative (OR), not cumulative (AND). **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XIX covers AI qualification for AIF investments. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The three paths are alternatives: (1) income ≥ 2 Cr, OR (2) net worth ≥ 7.5 Cr (3.75 Cr financial), OR (3) income ≥ 1 Cr + net worth ≥ 5 Cr (2.5 Cr financial). Failing one doesn't matter if another is met.
